description: 2-topic conversation, one clean switch, no return - baseline case

turns.question: Under which circumstances can a concern or complaint be made under this University of Essex whistleblowing policy?
Who can someone raise that concern with?
What constitutes plagiarism according to the academic offences procedure?
What penalties can be applied for that?

description: topic A, switch to B, explicit return to A - tests whether the contextualizer resolves the return to the correct distant topic, not the intervening one

turns.question: What does the Fitness to Study policy address in terms of student behavior?
What does the term 'Capped Mark' mean in the undergraduate rules of assessment?
Going back to my earlier question about student behavior concerns - who initiates that process?

description: ask about A, then explicitly compare to B - requires shifting focus while retaining awareness of A

turns.question: What is the maximum number of credits that can be carried forward for progression in the MSc Periodontology programme?
How does that compare to the minimum pass mark for East 15 Acting School's Masters programmes?
And what's the equivalent progression credit limit for that East 15 programme?

description: 3 topics with an explicit return to the FIRST after 2 intervening switches - longer-distance reference resolution

turns.question: Under which circumstances can a concern or complaint be made under this University of Essex whistleblowing policy?
How long do MBA students have to complete their studies at Essex?
What reassessment opportunities exist at the end of Year One for the Four-Year Honours Degree?
Earlier you mentioned the whistleblowing policy - is there a time limit on raising a concern under it?

description: 3 topics, return to the MIDDLE one (not the most recent, not the first) - the specific ambiguity class the real contextualizer-drift bug hit

turns.question: What constitutes plagiarism according to the academic offences procedure?
What is the maximum number of credits that can be carried forward for progression in the MSc Periodontology programme?
What's the minimum pass mark for all modules in East 15 Acting School's Masters programmes?
Going back to the periodontology question - does that credit limit apply the same way in Year 2 and Year 3?

description: switch between two RoA documents in DIFFERENT families (glossary vs 4yr rules) that share generic assessment vocabulary - tests whether the switch is clean or drags terminology from the wrong document

turns.question: What are the reassessment opportunities at the end of Year One for students under the Four-Year Honours Degree programme?
Separately, what does 'Capped Mark' mean in the glossary?
Does a capped mark ever apply to that Year One reassessment I asked about earlier?

description: 4 single-turn topics in a row, no follow-ups, no returns - stress test for whether rapid-fire topic changes confuse the contextualizer even without deep multi-turn history

turns.question: Under which circumstances can a concern or complaint be made under this University of Essex whistleblowing policy?
What does the Fitness to Study policy address in terms of student behavior?
How long do MBA students have to complete their studies at Essex?
What constitutes plagiarism according to the academic offences procedure?

description: 3 turns deep on one topic, switch away, then a distant return referencing something from turn 1 specifically (not the most recent turn on that topic)

turns.question: What is the maximum number of credits that can be carried forward for progression in the MSc Periodontology programme?
Is that limit the same for both the MSc and the Postgraduate Diploma route?
What happens if a student exceeds that trailing credit limit?
Switching topics - what's the minimum pass mark for all modules in East 15 Acting School's Masters programmes?
Back to the very first thing I asked about the credit limit - which department administers that programme?
